page: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor running at 3.9 GHz. It features 16 vCPUs across 8 physical cores with hyperthreading enabled, running on the Nitro hypervisor. The system is equipped with 64.0 GB of DDR5 memory operating at 7 GHz, providing 4.0 GB of RAM per core. It does not include local storage or dedicated GPUs, and provides a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps.

The x86_64 architecture includes advanced instruction sets such as Intel Advanced Matrix Extensions (AMX_TILE, AMX_INT8) and Advanced Vector Extensions (AVX-512). In benchmarks, these capabilities translate to strong single-threaded performance, with Geekbench single-core scores and gzip compression rates placing in the top 25%. Text processing benchmarks show top-tier performance in the top 10%. Multi-core operations, such as Geekbench multi-core and stress-ng multi-core, perform in the average range.

The DDR5 memory subsystem delivers an average cached read bandwidth of 1278.3 GB/sec and uncached read bandwidth of 115.07 GB/sec. For specialized workloads, the server achieves top-tier text generation speeds of 328.57 tokens/second on small 135M LLM models, though performance scales down to an average 15.39 tokens/second for medium 7B models. With its balanced resource allocation and lack of local storage, this instance is configured for general-purpose workloads, web serving, text processing, and memory-cached database applications.
description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose instance featuring an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs, 8 physical cores, and a 3.9 GHz clock speed. Running on the Nitro hypervisor, it is equipped with 64.0 GB of high-speed 7 GHz DDR5 memory, but lacks local storage and dedicated GPUs. The x86_64 architecture supports advanced instruction sets including Intel AMX and AVX-512. Benchmark data reveals strong single-threaded performance, particularly in text processing and gzip compression, alongside top-tier text generation speeds for small language models. Uncached memory read bandwidth reaches 115.07 GB/sec. With a baseline network bandwidth of 8 Gbps and a balanced 1:4 vCPU-to-RAM ratio, this server is configured for enterprise web serving, text processing pipelines, and general-purpose application hosting.
og_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server featuring 16 vCPUs, 64 GB of DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon processor, delivering strong single-core performance and advanced AMX instruction support.
meta_description: Amazon Web Services m8i.4xlarge is a general-purpose server with 16 vCPUs, 64 GB DDR5 RAM, and an Intel Xeon CPU, running on the Nitro hypervisor.
tagline: A balanced general-purpose server featuring high-speed DDR5 memory, advanced matrix extensions, and strong single-threaded processing capabilities.
bullet_points: Powered by an Intel Xeon 6975P-C processor with 16 vCPUs and 64 GB DDR5 RAM
Features Intel AMX and AVX-512 instruction sets for accelerated matrix operations
Delivers strong single-core performance in text processing and compression benchmarks
Provides 8 Gbps baseline network bandwidth without local storage
Applicable for web serving, text processing, and general-purpose enterprise applications
